name: Marriott Bonvoy Boundless® Credit Card
effective_cost: Low if you redeem the annual free night award

verdict.text: Keep if you'll actually book the annual free night; otherwise a mid-pack earner

earn_rates.category: Groceries, gas stations & dining (combined $6,000/yr cap, then 2×)

earn_note: The 3× categories share one combined $6,000/yr cap across groceries, gas, and dining, easy to blow through if you lean on any single bucket. After that cap (and everywhere else), this card earns a flat 2×, which beats Amex's Bonvoy cards' catch-all rate.

redemption_options.method: Transfer to airline miles (3:1, +5,000 bonus per 60,000 transferred)

points.note: Same Marriott Bonvoy currency and redemption math as Amex's Bonvoy cards. Points are worth the most redeemed for a free night you'd actually book, well above cashing out or transferring to airlines.

transfer_partners.highlight: Marriott Bonvoy transfers to 40+ airline programs at 3:1, with a 5,000-mile bonus for every 60,000 points transferred in a single transaction, but no hotel transfer partners, since it already is one.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: No confirmed recent changes to the airline transfer chart.

credits.name: Airline Incidental Credit
credits.subtitle: $50 × 2 · $250 min spend per half · thru 6/30/2027
credits.description: Up to $50 in statement credits twice a year (Jan–Jun and Jul–Dec) after spending $250 or more on eligible airline purchases within that half, active through June 30, 2027.
tips: Requires $250 of actual airline charges in each 6-month window; general travel spend doesn't count.
This is a limited-time promotional benefit, not a confirmed permanent feature; re-check before assuming it renews past mid-2027.

credits.name: Annual Free Night Award
credits.subtitle: up to 35,000-point category · after account anniversary
credits.description: One free night award each year on your account anniversary, redeemable at hotels with a redemption level at or under 35,000 Marriott Bonvoy points. Valid for 12 months from issuance.
tips: Lower cap than Amex's Bonvoy cards (50k/85k); aim for a property priced near the 35k ceiling to get full value.
$250 here is a conservative mid-range estimate, not a guarantee for any specific hotel.

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass + Credit
credits.subtitle: complimentary + $10/qtr non-restaurant · thru 2027
credits.description: Complimentary DashPass membership plus up to $10 off quarterly on non-restaurant DoorDash orders, active through December 31, 2027.
tips: Skip this if you're not already a DoorDash user; not worth chasing on its own.

protection_note: No trip cancellation/interruption and no rental car coverage, a real step down from Chase's own Sapphire Reserve and from Amex's Bonvoy cards. What's here (baggage, trip delay, purchase protection) is capped low ($500/item purchase protection, $500/traveler trip delay after a 12-hour trigger).
rental_note: No rental car coverage of any kind is stated for this card; use a different card for rentals.

status_perks.name: Marriott Bonvoy Silver Elite Status
status_perks.note: Complimentary with the card: 15 Elite Night Credits/year automatically, plus 1 more for every $5,000 spent. Silver is Bonvoy's entry tier; $35,000/yr in card spend unlocks Gold Elite.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

services.name: Visa Signature Concierge
services.detail: Complimentary 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and travel arrangements.

additional_cards.title: Additional Cards

benefits.text: Earns Marriott Bonvoy points on their spend (into your account)
Does not get their own Free Night Award or Elite status

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free: a way to pool household spend toward the 6× hotel rate and the $35,000 Gold Elite threshold.

timeline.text: Welcome offer changed to 125,000 points + 1 Free Night Award (up to 50k) after $3,000 spend in 3 months, replacing a temporary 5-Free-Night promotional offer that had run since Jan 8, 2026.
New $100/yr Airline Incidental Credit added ($50 semi-annually, $250 min spend per half), running through June 30, 2027.
